stories.title: Kendall Wells is 1 HR From History — Oklahoma Freshman Tied the All-Time Record at 37

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells has 37 home runs this season. That ties the all-time NCAA single-season record set by Arizona's Laura Espinoza in 1995.

One homer this week in Norman and history is hers. 🥎 #Sooners

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
In one season, Kendall Wells has broken:
• The NCAA freshman HR record (31)
• The SEC single-season HR record (27)
• The Oklahoma program record (35)
• Now tied the ALL-TIME NCAA record at 37

She's a freshman. #Sooners #SoftballTwitter

facebook.long_form:
🥎 ONE HOME RUN FROM HISTORY 🥎

Oklahoma freshman Kendall Wells needs just one more home run to own the most remarkable record in college softball history — the NCAA single-season home run record.

Wells hit her 37th homer of the season during the Sooners' Norman Regional last weekend, tying the mark set by Arizona's Laura Espinoza back in 1995. Let that sink in for a second: a record that has stood for 31 years is now within one swing of being erased.

And Wells didn't just sneak up on 37. She obliterated records along the way:

• Broke the NCAA freshman home run record at 31
• Set the SEC single-season home run record at 27
• Surpassed Jocelyn Alo to set the Oklahoma program record at 35
• Tied the all-time NCAA record at 37

She's a FRESHMAN. Batting close to .380. Leading the entire nation in home runs. And the stage could not be bigger — Oklahoma faces Mississippi State in the Super Regionals beginning Friday, May 22.

One homer and history is rewritten. We might be watching the greatest individual offensive season in college softball history, and it's not over yet.

[Tag a Sooners fan below who needs to be at Norman this weekend!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Kendall Wells sits at 37 home runs — tied with Arizona's Laura Espinoza (1995) for the all-time NCAA single-season record. One more in the Super Regional and the record is hers. The greatest freshman season in softball history might just be getting started. 🥎 #Sooners #SoftballTwitter

stories.title: Nebraska Hosts First-Ever Super Regional — Cornhuskers 49-6, WCWS Drought Ends Here?

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Nebraska has never hosted an NCAA Softball Super Regional.

Until Thursday.

The Cornhuskers are 49-6 and ready for their first home Super Regional, hosting Oklahoma State at 9 PM ET on ESPN2. #Huskers #GBR

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Nebraska's Jordy Frahm vs. Oklahoma State's Ruby Neylan.

Two of the best pitchers in college softball. Both from the Omaha area. Former travel ball teammates in high school.

They face each other in the Super Regional Thursday. #Huskers #OKState

facebook.long_form:
🌽 HISTORY IN LINCOLN 🌽

This Thursday, Nebraska softball will do something it has never done before in program history: host an NCAA Super Regional.

The Cornhuskers (49-6) have been almost perfect in 2026. They allowed just ONE run across all three of their regional games, shutting out every opponent until the final out. They've done it with elite pitching from Jordy Frahm and an offense that never seems to run dry.

And now? They get to do it at home.

But here's the storyline that makes this Super Regional truly special: Nebraska's Jordy Frahm will face Oklahoma State's Ruby Neylan in the pitching circle. Both are from the Omaha area. Both are considered among the best college pitchers in the country. And both once threw side-by-side as travel ball teammates in high school.

Now they're on opposite sides of a game that could end one program's 13-year WCWS drought.

Nebraska hasn't been to the Women's College World Series since 2013. Oklahoma State missed it just last season — ending a run they'd like to resume.

Game 1 is Thursday, May 21 at 9 PM ET on ESPN2. Something special is brewing in Lincoln.

[Who are you rooting for in this one?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Nebraska softball hosts its first-ever NCAA Super Regional this Thursday — and the Cornhuskers (49-6) are ready. A pitching matchup between two Omaha-area natives who were once travel ball teammates makes this one of the most compelling Super Regionals of the week. 🌽 #Huskers #GBR

article.headline: Nebraska Hosts Its First-Ever Super Regional — And Two Omaha Natives Make It Unforgettable

stories.title: Tennessee vs Georgia — All-SEC Super Regional, Lady Vols Host for Record 4th Straight Time

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Tennessee vs. Georgia. All-SEC Super Regional. Knoxville. Thursday at 7 PM ET.

Lady Vols (45-10) host a Super Regional for the 4th straight year — the first time that's happened in UT history. #LadyVols #GoDawgs

facebook.long_form:
🍊 ALL-SEC IN KNOXVILLE 🍊

When the NCAA brackets were revealed last week, the Tennessee-Georgia Super Regional jumped off the page: a battle of two SEC programs at Sherri Parker Lee Stadium for a trip to Oklahoma City.

Tennessee comes in at 45-10. The Lady Vols have been one of the sport's most consistent programs — and now they're hosting a Super Regional for the fourth consecutive year. That's never happened in program history.

For Tennessee, this is more than just a Super Regional. It's a program-defining week. They've made two WCWS trips in the last four seasons. Another appearance would cement this era as one of the best in Lady Vols softball history.

Georgia arrives at 41-18, hungry and battle-tested. Sophomore Addisen Fisher shut out Clemson 5-0 in the Regional Final — a dominant complete-game performance that should give Georgia real momentum heading north.

Game 1: Thursday, May 21 at 7 PM ET on ESPN2. Game 2: Friday, May 22 at 3 PM ET on ESPN2. If necessary, Game 3: Saturday, May 23 at 11 AM ET on ESPN.

An all-SEC showdown. A program milestone. One ticket to Oklahoma City. Let's go. 🥎

---
facebook.image_caption: Tennessee (45-10) hosts Georgia (41-18) in an all-SEC Super Regional beginning Thursday. The Lady Vols are hosting a Super Regional for the 4th consecutive year — the first time in program history. One team advances to the WCWS. 🍊 #LadyVols #GoDawgs

stories.title: Arkansas Hosts Duke — Razorbacks' 5th Super Regional, Still Searching for First WCWS Bid

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Arkansas has been to 5 NCAA Super Regionals. They've never made a WCWS.

Friday in Fayetteville, Courtney Deifel's Razorbacks get their best shot yet. Duke (43-15) is the obstacle. #WPS #GoDuke

facebook.long_form:
🐗 ARKANSAS' UNFINISHED BUSINESS 🐗

Five Super Regionals. Five losses. That's the Arkansas softball story under head coach Courtney Deifel — a program that has done everything right except reach the Women's College World Series.

The Razorbacks are back for a sixth try.

Arkansas (45-11) dominated the Fayetteville Regional, winning every game by run rule. The combined score across three games: 27-3. They're playing some of the best softball of the Deifel era, and Bogle Park is one of the most hostile environments in the sport.

Standing in their way: No. 12 seed Duke. The Blue Devils (43-15) are one of college softball's great consistent programs — this is their FOURTH Super Regional appearance in the last five years. They came through an elimination gauntlet at the Durham Regional, losing to Arizona before beating Marshall, then Arizona twice in a row to advance.

Duke doesn't just show up. They find a way to win.

Something has to give Friday morning in Fayetteville. Arkansas chasing their first WCWS bid. Duke refusing to go home quietly. This is exactly the kind of matchup that defines the Super Regional round.

Game 1: Friday, May 22 at 11 AM ET on ESPNU. Game 2: Saturday, May 23 at 4 PM ET on ESPN2.

[Can Arkansas finally break through?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Arkansas has reached 5 NCAA Super Regionals and never made the WCWS. That could change this weekend at Bogle Park, where the Razorbacks host No. 12 Duke (43-15) beginning Friday. Arkansas has unfinished business. 🐗 #WPS #GoDuke

article.headline: Arkansas Has Never Reached the WCWS. Duke Won't Make It Easy to Get There.

stories.title: Texas vs Arizona State — Longhorns Host ASU's First Super Regional Since 2022

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
No. 2 Texas has made 7 consecutive NCAA Super Regionals. They've looked unstoppable in 2026 — three regional wins, each by 7+ runs.

Arizona State didn't get the memo. The Sun Devils open Friday in Austin. #HookEm #SunDevils

facebook.long_form:
🤘 SEVEN STRAIGHT FOR TEXAS 🤘

When the NCAA Tournament started, Texas didn't blink. The Longhorns defeated Wagner, Wisconsin, and Baylor in Austin — winning each game by at least seven runs. No drama, no close calls, no sweat.

Now comes the real test.

No. 2 Texas (45-10) hosts Arizona State in a Super Regional beginning Friday night at McCombs Field. For the Longhorns, it's the seventh consecutive Super Regional appearance — a run of postseason consistency that few programs in any sport can match.

Arizona State arrives with a chip on their shoulder. The Sun Devils are back in the Super Regionals for the first time since 2022, and they announced their arrival in style — a 9-1 run-rule victory over regional host Texas A&M in College Station. That's not a team sneaking into the field. That's a team that belongs there.

The Sun Devils will face the defending program that never lets up. This one could surprise people.

Game 1: Friday, May 22 at 9 PM ET on ESPN2. Game 2: Saturday, May 23 at 7 PM ET on ESPN.

Seven straight or first stumble? Find out Friday night. 🥎

---
facebook.image_caption: No. 2 Texas makes its 7th consecutive Super Regional appearance Friday, hosting Arizona State at McCombs Field. The Longhorns haven't lost a step, but the Sun Devils are back in the field for the first time since 2022 and making noise. 🤘 #HookEm #SunDevils

article.headline: Texas Makes It Seven Straight — Arizona State Arrives in Austin Ready to Interrupt the Streak

stories.title: Super Regionals Picks — Our 8 WCWS Predictions Before First Pitch Thursday

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Super Regional picks before Thursday's first pitch:

✅ Alabama ✅ Oklahoma ✅ Texas ✅ Nebraska
✅ Tennessee ✅ Arkansas

Upset watch: Duke over Arkansas? UCF over UCLA?

Who's your WCWS pick? 🥎

facebook.long_form:
🗓️ SUPER REGIONAL PREDICTIONS 🗓️

Super Regional play begins Thursday. Before first pitch, here are our picks — and the games we're watching closest:

**Our WCWS Bracket Picks:**
🏆 Alabama over LSU (No. 1 seed, Vic Moten is lights-out)
🏆 Oklahoma over Mississippi State (Wells needs one homer for history)
🏆 Texas over Arizona State (7 straight — they know how to win this round)
🏆 Nebraska over Oklahoma State (49-6, home field, Frahm vs Neylan)
🏆 Tennessee over Georgia (Lady Vols at home have been dominant)
🏆 Florida over Texas Tech (Gators have home field and depth)
🏆 UCF over UCLA (fresh off ending FSU's dynasty — believe in the magic)
🏆 Arkansas over Duke (the WCWS drought ends in Fayetteville)

**Biggest upset watch:**
⚠️ Duke over Arkansas — Blue Devils have tournament pedigree and nothing to lose
⚠️ Arizona State over Texas — the Sun Devils could be the hottest team in the bracket

Who reaches Devon Park? We'll find out May 21-24. WCWS begins May 28 in Oklahoma City.

[Drop your bracket predictions below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Super Regionals begin Thursday and eight teams will fight for eight spots at the WCWS. Alabama, Oklahoma, and Texas look like the class of the field — but upsets are coming. Who's your pick? 🥎 #Softball #WCWS2026
